symptoms.symptom_name: Severe crampy pain with explosive diarrhoea and visceral hypersensitivity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Crampy pain, diarrhoea and marked visceral hypersensitivity fit a post-infectious IBS/mast cell flare.
Diarrhoea is non-specific; afebrile with no contacts lowers infection likelihood.

symptoms.symptom_name: Presyncope/tachycardia/dry mucosa; cannot keep fluids down

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural faintness, tachycardia and dry mucosa with inability to keep fluids down indicate significant dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft (not rigid) abdomen, passing stool/flatus, no GI bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No peritonism, passage of stool/flatus and no bleeding argue against a surgical abdomen, though examination is needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no sick contacts, reproducible non-infective pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with no contacts and a reproducible pattern lowers infective probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chronic alarm features (weight loss, persistent GI bleeding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chronic alarm features lowers the likelihood of serious organic disease, though red flags must be monitored.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft distended abdomen, diffuse tenderness, hyperactive bowel sounds, no peritonism (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypermotility with a soft, non-peritonitic abdomen fits a functional flare.
No peritonism on exam argues against peritonitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-infectious onset (after food poisoning 6 months ago)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Onset after gastroenteritis is characteristic of post-infectious IBS with mast cell reactivity.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Post-infectious IBS / mast cell flare with dehydration
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A severe post-infectious IBS/mast cell flare with visceral hypersensitivity, afebrile and non-peritonitic, but with presyncope, tachycardia and inability to keep fluids down in an isolated patient, needs urgent in-person/ED assessment for IV rehydration and to exclude a surgical abdomen — not remote injections.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person abdominal assessment and postural vitals
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude a surgical abdomen and quantify dehydration; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: IV rehydration and bloods (U&E, FBC, CRP)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To correct volume depletion and screen for infection/renal impairment.
If clinical features suggest an infective cause.
